description: 15 test cases covering mechanical assertions and human assertions for the buyers-guide skill. Mechanical assertions can be checked programmatically against the output document. Human assertions require review.

tests.name: Research depth — retailer-exclusive products
tests.prompt: Best prebuilt PC with RTX 5080 and Ryzen 9800X3D, budget under $3,000, US, gaming and streaming at 1440p
tests.failure_mode: Track A stops at editorial-list products, misses Micro Center
human_assertions: Micro Center option is present in the guide
Track A search log shows at least one retailer-specific search was run (Micro Center, Costco, or similar)

tests.name: Regional sources — UK appliances
tests.failure_mode: Track C uses Consumer Reports (US-only); retailers are US
human_assertions: Which? or IEC test results are cited for spec verification
All retailers are UK-appropriate (AO.com, Currys, etc.) — no US-only retailers

tests.name: Regional sources — AU non-electronics
tests.prompt: Best standing desks under AU$800, Australia
tests.failure_mode: Track C uses US sources; retailers are US
human_assertions: AU retailers are present (e.g. Autonomous AU, local options)
Wobble test or stability measurement sources are cited

tests.name: Safety recall + pool count
tests.prompt: Best espresso machines under €400, Germany — [context: one top product has an active EU RAPEX recall]
tests.failure_mode: Recalled product is included in ranked list; pool drops below 3 without informing user
human_assertions: RAPEX recall notice is linked in the What To Avoid card
If pool drops below 3 after exclusion, user is informed before document generation begins

tests.name: All finalists below 6.0
tests.failure_mode: Ratings are inflated; normal positive intro is written despite poor category quality
human_assertions: Final Recommendations are framed as least-bad picks, not genuine endorsements
A budget increase recommendation is included with a specific threshold

tests.name: Mid-research requirement change
tests.prompt: Best power tools under $200, Canada — then mid-conversation: 'must be cordless only'
tests.failure_mode: Skill silently continues with pre-change candidate list including corded products
human_assertions: Affected corded products are removed from the candidate list
New cordless-only candidates are searched for
User explicitly confirms the updated list before document generation proceeds

tests.name: Refresh protocol
tests.prompt: Refresh my 6-month-old robot vacuum guide, US — [provide mock original guide as context]
tests.failure_mode: Skips Track C spot-check; skips recall search; does not note updated date in banner
human_assertions: Track C spot-check is performed for each product (current manufacturer spec page fetched)
Recall search is run for each product
New entrants are checked via Track A with current date

tests.name: Contradictory requirements
tests.prompt: Best gaming monitors under $150, US — must be 4K OLED 144Hz
tests.failure_mode: Skill proceeds to research despite impossible requirements at this budget
human_assertions: The specific conflict is explained (4K OLED 144Hz does not exist under $150)
Options to relax requirements are offered with a ranked recommendation

tests.name: New product — no price history
tests.prompt: Best [product launched 4 weeks ago, construct a scenario] under $X, US
tests.failure_mode: Price History row is omitted or contains a guessed value; community reception marked N/A when early reports exist
human_assertions: Community reception is scored from early reports if any exist — not marked N/A unless truly nothing found
Weaknesses section notes price stability is unknown

tests.name: Dominant winner
tests.failure_mode: Intro does not name the dominant winner; false parity between options is implied
human_assertions: The dominant product (e.g. Sony WH-1000XM5) is named in the intro paragraph with the rating gap noted
Final Recommendations lead with 'if you only read one recommendation' framing for the top product

tests.name: Naming variant trap
tests.prompt: Best gaming monitors under $500, US — [category with near-identical model names e.g. AOC Q27GAZD vs Q27G4ZD]
tests.failure_mode: Wrong ASIN is linked; a similar model name is recommended instead of the correct one
human_assertions: Linked pages match the model described in the guide (title on the product page matches the recommended model name)

tests.name: Compatibility guide — Step 1.5
tests.prompt: Best MagSafe power banks for my iPhone 17 Pro Max with OtterBox Defender Series Pro case
tests.failure_mode: Skips Step 1.5 hardware verification; does not confirm MagSafe compatibility with the specific case model
human_assertions: OtterBox Defender Series Pro MagSafe compatibility is confirmed before research begins
Variant-level verification of the case model is documented

tests.name: Stretch pick
tests.prompt: Best 4K monitors under $400, US — [where the best option is $449]
tests.failure_mode: Over-budget product is included without a stretch pick flag
human_assertions: Stretch pick framing appears in the card copy with the percentage over budget stated
The stretch pick is not presented as equal to in-budget options

tests.name: Hardest combined scenario — UK standing desk refresh
tests.prompt: Refresh my standing desk guide from 6 months ago, UK — [provide mock original guide with one discontinued product and one new entrant]
tests.failure_mode: Misses UK non-Amazon price history; uses US Track C sources; skips spot-check; does not update rankings when order changes
human_assertions: UK retailers are used (Fully UK, Autonomous UK, Fleksispot, etc.) — no US-only retailers
Track C uses furniture-appropriate sources (wobble tests, stability measurements)
Rankings table, verdict badges, and Final Recommendations are updated if ranking order changed
Track C spot-check is performed for each retained product

tests.name: Baseline correctness
tests.prompt: Best wireless gaming headsets under $150, US
tests.failure_mode: Any mechanical rule violation on a clean, uncomplicated request
human_assertions: All mechanical assertions pass
Guide reads coherently as a real product recommendation document
